title: Betl Oxtroe

{{Quote|Sadly, the first round of talks had only begun when the Admiral fell victim to an assassin's blade, presumably wielded by a Noghri, and the plan was forgotten.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="des" />}}
'''Betl Oxtroe''' was one of a select few [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] to ascend the [[Military rank/Legends|ranks]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], becoming an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]. After the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] entered a second phase in the wake of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Oxtroe remained loyal to [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] as many senior officers broke away to establish themselves as [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]].

A proponent of peace, Oxtroe attempted to broker a truce between the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the remnants of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. She proposed the creation of a parliamentary monarchy with eleven-year-old [[Ederlathh Pallopides/Legends|Ederlathh Pallopides]], a distant grand-niece of Palpatine, as a possible head, in return for immunity for former [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] personnel. Admiral Oxtroe died at the blade of a [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] before the first round of secret negotiations with the New Republic were completed, and the idea was forgotten.

==Biography==
Betl Oxtroe was an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], one<ref name="des" /> of few high-[[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] [[Gender/Legends|women]] in the armed forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The Empire fell to pieces, with swaths of Imperials turning [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and establishing their own dominions in various parts of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Meanwhile on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], Palpatine's successors advocated the continuation of the war with the rebels, who had reformed into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Despite the fragmentation, Oxtroe and her forces remained loyal under the command of [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], alongside other officers such as [[Uther Kermen]] and [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], and she was responsible for the defense of several [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] and [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Admiral Oxtroe was one of the few Imperial proponents for a peace process with the New Republic, espousing a parliamentary monarchy that would unite both sides of the conflict. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Oxtroe made secret entreaties to the New Republic, hoping to open talks of establishing such a system. Palpatine had a distant grand-niece, [[Ederlathh Pallopides/Legends|Ederlathh Pallopides]],<ref name="des" /> who Oxtroe proposed as a possible heir to the late [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].<ref name="cswe" /> Pallopides was just eleven years old, so she would act as a mere figurehead; the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] would have control, playing a similar role to the former [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisors]] under Palpatine. In return for what Oxtroe saw as Imperial concessions, the Provisional Council would give amnesty to the members of the former [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. The New Republic was open to negotiations, and talks began in earnest.<ref name="des" />

Many Imperials did not want peace accords, furthermore the reborn Emperor Palpatine was preparing for his return, while [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] was hiding in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], waiting for the right time to [[Thrawn campaign|strike]] at the New Republic.<ref name="des" /> Thrawn commanded a [[Species/Legends|species]] of fierce warriors known as the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]]; a Noghri [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] killed Oxtroe before the first round of talks had concluded,<ref name="cswe" /> and the idea was not pursued. New Republic [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] suspected Thrawn's hand in the Admiral's death, but had no evidence.<ref name="des" /> Ultimately, the remnants of the Empire and New Republic continued to wage war for over a decade, until [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] and [[Ponc Gavrisom]] signed [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|accords]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].<ref name="nec" />

==Personality and traits==
Betl Oxtroe was one<ref name="des" /> of a few women to achieve a high rank in the Empire. Females, though accepted into Imperial Military forces, were almost never given promotions, and [[Natasi Daala]] was the first woman to achieve the rank of admiral. Despite facing this uphill struggle,<ref name="negtc" /> Oxtroe was promoted to that same rank,<ref name="des" /> making her the joint highest-ranking woman in Imperial service.<ref name="cswe" />

Oxtroe wanted peace after Palpatine's death and was not averse to communicating with the New Republic, unlike most of her colleagues; she did not defect, however. Instead, Oxtroe put forth an innovative plan to make peace while still maintaining some semblance of the Empire she served.<ref name="des" />

==Behind the scenes==
Betl Oxtroe was created by [[Michael Allen Horne]] for the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', published in [[1993]], and later referenced in the multi-author ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[2008]]. In [[2012]], she received a mention in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]].

Oxtroe was briefly mentioned in an early draft of ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Abel G. Peña]]. Originally due to be published in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine under the title ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', after the cancellation of ''Gamer'' the article was renamed and condensed to be published in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]].<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref><ref name="DD:TIGA">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

{{Cutstart}}
In the earlier draft, Oxtroe is established to have been a member of the [[Order of the Canted Circle]], a [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] secret society, created by [[Paul Sudlow]] for ''[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]'' in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 9|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 9]]. An unwritten order rule dictated that no more than eleven members could be inducted within each decade, but Oxtroe's ordination was the twelfth of the decade starting [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]. Grand Admirals [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] and Thrawn had become additional exemptions by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].<ref name="DD:TIGA" />
{{Cutend}}
